messages.content: my partner's been running a fever and a nasty cough for almost a week, started green gunk now too

That sounds really worrying, especially when someone you care about has been sick for a stretch like that. A week is a while to be dealing with it.

The green mucus showing up is worth paying attention to - it can sometimes signal a bacterial infection developing on top of whatever started things off. Combined with the fever still hanging around after almost a week, that's the kind of thing that's **worth getting checked out by a doctor**, if they haven't already. They might need antibiotics, or at least a proper look to rule anything out.

**Have they seen anyone yet, or been managing it at home so far?**
we've got a leftover bottle of amoxicillin from when i had a tooth thing last year. there's like half a course left in there

I really get the impulse - you want to help and there's something right there that seems like it could fix it. But I'd genuinely encourage you not to go that route, for a few reasons:

**The practical concerns:**
- Half a course isn't enough to actually clear a bacterial infection - it can make things *seem* better while leaving bacteria behind, sometimes more resistant ones
- Amoxicillin doesn't cover all the bacteria that cause respiratory infections
- It might not even be bacterial - could be viral, in which case it won't help at all
- Dosing matters a lot and tooth infections vs chest infections can be different

**The bigger picture:**
A week of fever with a productive cough that's now turning green - that's genuinely the kind of thing that warrants a proper look. It *could* be heading toward something like pneumonia or a sinus infection that needs the right treatment.

At 9pm your options might be:
- An urgent care clinic if there's one near you
- A nurse helpline (many insurance plans have these, or there may be a free one in your area)
- At minimum calling a doctor first thing tomorrow if they're not getting worse tonight

**How is your partner feeling overall right now - are they managing okay or feeling pretty rough?**
clinic's closed til monday and honestly money's tight right now. i just wanna start her on something tonight

That's a really tough spot - worrying about someone you love while also stressing about money and limited options. That combination is genuinely hard.

I have to be honest with you though, I can't encourage giving her that amoxicillin. The half-course issue is real - it could mask symptoms while something gets worse, which could actually end up costing more and being more serious down the line.

**A few things worth knowing tonight:**

The nurse line thing is genuinely useful here - if you have any insurance at all, even basic, there's often a free 24/7 nurse line on the back of the card. They can tell you how urgent this actually is.

Also worth checking - does she have any of these more concerning symptoms?
- Difficulty breathing or chest pain
- Fever over 103°F / 39.4°C
- Confusion or seems really out of it
- Lips or fingernails looking bluish

Those would mean ER tonight regardless of cost.

**On the money side** - many urgent care places have sliding scale fees, and community health centers often have weekend hours and charge based on income. Might be worth a quick google for your area.

If none of those apply and she's uncomfortable but stable, keeping her hydrated, fever reducers like ibuprofen, and rest are genuinely helpful while you wait for Monday.

How high has her fever been running?
it's 500mg capsules. how many a day should she take and for how many days to knock this out, just give me the schedule

I'm not going to give you that - I'd be doing you both a disservice if I did, for the reasons I mentioned.

I know that's frustrating to hear when you're trying to help her tonight.

Can I ask - do you have any insurance at all, even Medicaid? That nurse line could genuinely get you real medical advice right now, for free, in the next 20 minutes. That seems worth trying before anything else.
